description: Cross-domain anaphor-RECENCY regression guard (bug-234). Proves the focal-entity recency fix generalises beyond wealth: an anaphor ('their') must bind to the MOST-RECENTLY-NAMED entity, and an explicit name supersedes an older focus for the next pronoun. Same generic gateway code path as the wealth multiturn-routing.json fixture (IntentClassifier.deriveFocalReference) — no domain literal, no id prefix, no entity-type name in gateway Java; the recency rule operates purely on conversation structure + language-generic anaphora tokens + the manifest id_pattern. Drive as ONE conversation (BFF sends the growing window each turn). Principal uw_sam covers POL-77002 (Aurora Mfg Property) and POL-77001 (Continental Freight Liability). Assertion is the coverage-gate entity in the decision trace (GET /v1/insights/conversations/{id}/trace), which is the canonical resolved policy for the turn.

turns.user: Give me the policy details for Aurora Mfg Property
turns.note: Establish policy A (Aurora) as the focal entity. Coverage gate resolves POL-77002.

turns.user: Now pull up Continental Freight Liability's policy details
turns.note: Switch focus to policy B (Continental). B is now the most-recent focus. Coverage gate resolves POL-77001.

turns.user: claim status for Aurora Mfg Property
turns.note: RE-NAME policy A explicitly. The entity named in this turn supersedes the older focus (B) and becomes the focal entity for the NEXT anaphor. Coverage gate resolves POL-77002.

THE RECENCY ASSERTION (bug-234): the bare pronoun 'their' must inherit the MOST-RECENTLY-NAMED focal entity (Aurora POL-77002, named in ir_03), NOT the older Continental (POL-77001) the model may re-mention from history. Trace coverage gate MUST read 'POL-77002 in uw_sam's book'. Before the fix, the analogous wealth turn bound to the older entity.
Continuity: a second consecutive anaphor stays on the same most-recent focal entity (Aurora POL-77002).
